programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Live from Manchester, Michelle Ackerley finds out how the UK Football Policing Unit keeps fans and the public safe. In the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest appeals and CCTV(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Trading Standards Officers investigate a travel company boss conning customers, while officers examine a sewage leak which polluted a stream running through an Oxfordshire village(n)

programme.desc: Emma has to deal with an awkward situation involving a patient, Karen hears some gossip, and Ruhma finds herself caught up at the hospital(n)

programme.title: Impossible

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in the Devon countryside, helping a mother and daughter duo with £750,000 find a home for three generations of their family and an eco-camping business. The presenter also visits Chagford Community Farm, one of the UK's first community-supported micro-dairies and sees how residents are readily signing up to buying its local produce(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden for Neil and Jack, two friends living together in Leeds who want a space to entertain their guests(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Adrian Edmondson, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd and Jordan Stephens to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at the Ford factory in Dagenham, Essex(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: Driven: The Billy Monger Story
programme.desc: Documentary following 18-year-old Billy Monger as he attempts to get back into motor racing after being involved in a crash in April 2017 that resulted in the amputation of his legs. With the support of the racing community and his hero Lewis Hamilton, Billy will have to change the rules of motorsport and learn to drive again without legs, all while coming to terms with life as a person with a disability(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines how supermarkets are fighting to bring consumers their food on demand, and finds out how Ocado are using robots to revolutionise online delivery. Elsewhere in the programme, he also visits a state of the art lettuce farm in Norfolk which supplies 300 million lettuces a year, and Nikki Fox tests out the Co-op's new pay in the aisle technology as we move towards a checkout-free future(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley meets the Yorkshire and Humber Marine Unit in Whitby, and Rav Wilding presents appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a flat in Forest Hill, London, and two terraced houses in Leeds and Nantwich, Cheshire, before speaking to their new owners(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers investigate the sale of knives to under-18s. They also catch up with a Lamborghini-driving fraudster who has so many aliases he loses track of who he is supposed to be(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: An animal-lover falls down a sewer trying to rescue his dog and a 60-year-old woman trying to row the Atlantic finds herself in jeopardy when her boat fills with water(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and Nick Hall help two teams scour Nottingham racecourse for items, while presenter Charlie Ross tests people's knowledge of hallmarks(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma finds herself in an awkward situation, Zara gets annoyed at everyone talking and Rob is taken aback when a woman walks into the station and confesses to burning down her house(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin visits Shropshire in search of a country home for a pair of free spirits who have a £300,000 budget with which to finally lay down some roots. Jonnie also gets in the saddle on a ride through the Shropshire hills with a bespoke bike builder(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Arit Anderson compete for the chance to bring a taste of the Mediterranean to Pat and Jeremy's boring square garden in Shoreham-by-Sea, West Sussex(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst's trip takes in the Shires, where Tim shares some interesting sandwich recipes and uncovers an old coffin, while Catherine finds some medals(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby is left distraught after Rainie reveals the truth about her night with Ian and decides to leave the Square. But worse is to come. Daniel tries to distract Jean ahead of her operation, Chantelle's nerves threaten her chances of winning the competition and Phil overhears Sonia speaking to Bianca about barely seeing her and sends them an anonymous gift(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox return with the consumer show. They investigate allegations about a safety device in millions of homes and rank energy companies on cost, complaints and customer service. Pixie Lott exposes how high street chains are failing on the basics of recycling, and in Rogue Traders, Matt confronts a gardening firm whose elaborate lies have left customers thousands of pounds out of pocket(n)

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: 'Odd One Out'
programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. In this personal documentary, the singer opens up about the impact cyberbullies had on her life and the effect on her mental health. Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second edition of the topical debate - originally billed as the first of a new series until another episode was sneaked in last week. As usual she will be inviting the studio audience - this time a venue in Norwich - to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: When Andrew Neil decided to step down from This Week back in July, the BBC decided that rather than continue with a new host, they would find a different show to take its place. And here it is - although fans of Radio 5 Live will already be familiar with Brexitcast, which has become hugely popular since its launch in 2017. This TV version sees the same team, Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason, engaging in light-hearted gossip about all the latest goings-on in the process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley meets the volunteer teams who carry out rescues in the caves and mines of the Peak District in Derbyshire. Rav Wilding has the latest appeals and CCTV footage(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, to see a commercial space, and in Kent meets a couple who are returning to property development after taking a few years out. Dion Dublin meets a husband and wife team at a split-level flat in London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Trading Standards Officers search the home of a loan shark and find a safe containing thousands of pounds. They also force a rogue builder to pay compensation to his elderly victims. Narrated by Harry Hepple(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles introduces more footage of near misses caught on CCTV, meeting the people who survived the serious accidents. This episode features a paraglider who crashed into a wall in the Peak District, a cyclist who smashed face-first onto the pavement witnessed by her son, and a cameraman who while filming a storm almost ended up a victim himself(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles starts this programme at the British Bird of Prey centre in the National Botanic Garden of Wales and asks the people of Carmarthenshire to help identify a mystery object. The red and blue teams then go shopping with experts Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt, hoping to find items of value to take to auction in Cardiff(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara tries to be the bigger person when she finds out about Daniel and Becky's plans, while Valerie helps her neighbour when his selfless act is tarnished by the ultimate betrayal(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson crosses the Solent to property shop on the Isle of Wight with a couple who have a £750,000 budget to find their dream island home with holiday-let potential. During his journey, the presenter also gets an intimate glimpse into 19th-century royal life when he visits Osbourne House, one of Queen Victoria's favourite homes(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold Hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are joined by Arit Anderson to create an all-year-round garden in Byfleet, Surrey, for stylish octogenarian Gay to enjoy the birds and remember her late husband. As ever, the two design teams go head to head, so it remains to be seen whther newcomer Arit's garden, which uses repeating circle motifs to create interest in an otherwise small and featureless space and incorporates a curved wooden seat surrounded by bright flowers, will triumph(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England goalkeeper David James, Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Elinor Barker, Ryder Cup golfer Andy Sullivan and rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is unsure how to deal with Bobby and things only get worse when Max tells him he has agreed to let his son stay with him. Chantelle makes it to the final two in the competition - only to face a big decision. After her operation, Jean is overjoyed when Kat gets Stacey on the phone. But then Daniel arrives with devastating news(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The battle for a semi-final place reaches its climax as the four celebrities are tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by last year's finalists Martin Bayfield and Spencer Matthews and champion John Partridge. Only the best two contestants can come one step closer to winning the title of Celebrity MasterChef 2019. For those who don't make the grade, the journey ends here(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Mike and Karen try to get their relationship back on track after Mike's crab stall misdemeanour, while he finally finds Bigsy sleeping rough at the castle after going Awol. Meanwhile after the police visit Marion about the break in next door, Karen heads back to the salon to discover that Geraldine is under suspicion over the mystery of what happened to the contents of Mrs Bookham's handbag. Comedy written by Benidorm's Derren Litten, starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with Lucy Porter, Romesh Ranganathan and Judge Robert Rinder competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including lavish proposals, school reunions and New Year's Eve(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Steve McAndrew brings his dying father to London so he can spend his final days in a nearby hospice, but how much time he can spend with his parent is anyone's guess - the Ucos team is hard at work investigating the cold case murder of 55-year-old interpreter Agnes Bradley. A DNA sample provides a link to a rebellious teenager, and the team wonders if the killer could be related to him. But a whole new can of worms is opened when investigators discover the lad's mother was raped - could the same person have killed Agnes? Gerry and Steve hope that interviewing her friends from a local chess club may shed new light on the matter, but it's a possible professional issue that could hold the key to unlocking the mystery. Denis Lawson and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a tranquil garden in Newport, Wales, which must be suitable for growing herbs and practicing meditation(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the New Forest and Southampton Water, and learn about tide milling. Items under the hammer include a mantle clock and a pocket watch(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Kirsten O'Brien takes a 15-mile walk along the River Tee, exploring the history of Upper Teesdale, meeting some of extraordinary people who call its banks home and taking a wild swim in its white waters. She begins her trek at South Gare near Redcar - a childhood playground for Kirsten and her brother - and then takes in High Force, Middleton and ancient Egglestone Abbey(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: The story of Syrian refugee families who have made their homes on the island of Bute in the Firth of Clyde, finding a place of safety after the horrors of the Syrian war they have escaped. Mounzer runs the Orient Salon, named after the busy barber shop he ran in his native Damascus, while next door Rahaf and her husband Fadel run a restaurant and takeaway bringing a taste of Syria to Bute. Morven Christie narrates(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Families from Devon and Kent exchange homes and lifestyles in an experience that will put the way they live to the test. After the swap, they reveal whether they enjoyed themselves and what they have learned(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives investigate the affairs of a former insurance worker who has died leaving a valuable estate in Battersea, south London. Meanwhile, the case of a bus fanatic reveals the story of how a band of eagle-eyed servicemen defended Britain's shores(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary following workers at Rolls-Royce's Derby factory as they construct the company's Trent 1000 jet engine in preparation for its maiden flight attached to a Boeing 787 Dreamliner. The film explores the technology involved in its construction, and charts the ups and downs of working on the assembly line. Narrated by Gerard Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: First of a two-part programme catching up with zookeeper Giles Clark and the Sumatran tiger cubs he was hand-rearing in his home in the suburbs of Queensland. Spot and Stripe are approaching their first birthdays and Giles has his hands full guiding them from adolescence into adulthood, while Spot faces uncertainty about his eyesight and his relationship with Stripe. The Clarks also make a big decision to safeguard the cub's wild cousins, with Giles, his wife Kerri and son Kynan embarking on a road trip through Indonesia with the aim of helping to protect endangered species(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, experts Thomas Plant and Catherine Southon choose items for auction, including a collection of First World War memorabilia and an array of postcards(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: The team trial a ground-breaking new technique as they help rehabilitate a pair of orphaned seals, and have their hands full dealing with a pair of orphaned barn owl chicks(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Restorers get to work on a grandfather clock, hoping to make it chime again, and doctors perform surgery on a teddy bear with only one ear and one arm after a run-in with a puppy(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The five remaining contestants take part in a commercial design challenge to transform holiday lodges in the Lincolnshire woods. Split into a team of two and a team of three, the competitors embark on the biggest project of the series so far, designing a bedroom each, along with a shared kitchen-diner and an outdoor area. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: In a radical social experiment based on a scheme in Germany, 14 young volunteers between the ages of 18 and 24 spend four weeks delivering patient care on the NHS frontline at Royal Derby Hospital, aiming to find out whether volunteers could help relieve the pressures of staff on the wards and make a positive difference to patient care in the NHS. This time, the volunteers embark on their second week at Royal Derby Hospital, where some participants struggle to deal with the difficult realities of life and death. Michael and Piotr work alongside the emergency team to assist an elderly patient who has fallen in her garden, while Alishba offers support to the mother of a young boy who has been brought in for an MRI(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Taking the children to visit her Aunt and Uncle in Vancouver, Sam learns an upsetting family secret. Starring Pamela Adlon, Olivia Edward, Hannah Alligood and Mikey Madison. Written by Pamela Adlon and Louis CK(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass re-investigate the case of Edward Devlin and Alfred Burns, who were arrested and sentenced to death for the 1951 murder of Liverpudlian Beatrice Rimmer(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: Investigating conspiracy theories surrounding multi-billionaire George Soros and the allegations of his secret plots to overthrow governments and flood countries with migrants(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Thousands of people across Britain are being forced into human slavery by ruthless gangs. This Panorama special follows West Midlands Police as they bring down the biggest ever caught. The gang held hundreds of people captive, set them to work, stole their wages, made them eat from skips, wash in canals and threatened to kill them if they tried to escape. The programme hears unique first-hand testimony from slaves who have waived their lifelong right to anonymity so they can tell their stories. It also features exclusive access to the police and legal team who have spent more than four years bringing gang members to justice(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: The inaugural Dagenham Sunday Market Food Awards take place, and two new traders show off their wares - edible cakes for dogs and wooden chainsaw sculptures(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: A look back at some of the biggest characters from the series, seeing whether the risk taking new traders are still in business, who has shuffled back to the day job, and who is making a killing? Plus, the five golden rules of trading are revealed. Narrated by Larry Lamb(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In the Highlands, new entrant crofters Lynn and Sandra welcome more new sheep and take delivery of a batch of hens that they hope will lay the foundations of an 'egg empire'. In Northern Ireland, the Rodgers host a barbecue for the local Young Farmers Club, but it is no ordinary flame-grilled affair, while Raymond and Johnny gather the flock to wean this year's lambs in the Cairngorms(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Families from Maida Vale and Warwickshire put their homes and lifestyles under the microscope as they exchange properties with complete strangers(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Research into Eileen Holmes' family leads to a founding member of the SAS. Meanwhile, the case of Audrey Barton reveals a relative honoured for his contribution to the country(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Workers at British defence contractor QinetiQ discuss some of their secretive projects, including scientists and engineers who are building robots to defuse Afghanistan's deadly roadside bombs - as well as adapting them to help in dangerous civilian situations in the UK. The programme also explores a project to make wind turbines less visible to radar, and follows the work of specialists racing to get Chinook helicopters ready for front-line service. Narrated by Gerard Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Clarks see the harsh reality of poaching in Indonesia when they visit tigers that were caught in snares, prompting Giles to concoct an ambitious plan to save the injured big cats in the wild. Back in Australia, Spot and Stripe are days away from their first birthday and will no longer be classed as cubs, while Spot faces an operation that may end with him losing all vision(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Previously unseen finds from the show's travels on visits to Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, the Bowes Museum in Co Durham, the Grand Pier in Weston-super-Mare in Somerset and Norwich Cathedral. The antiques experts include Caroline Hawley, Kate Bateman, Thomas Plant, David Harper and Jonathan Pratt with items ranging from Black Forest carvings to a delicate oil painting completed on glass by the artist Bessie Bamber. Presenter Paul Martin also takes a look at the lavish interiors and the beautiful gardens at Powis Castle in mid-Wales(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: Staff travel to the north of Shetland to release a male otter that has been rehabilitated at the centre, while chief vet Romain Pizzi has to draw on all his years of experience and skill as he tries to save a vixen with a broken leg(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores two Royal Worcester vases that are precious family heirlooms, along with a Cirencester village clock and a 19th-century hay press(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King embark on a 2,000-mile journey along the famous American road, taking them through eight different states, each with their own history, flavours and people. They begin by learning about Chicago's meatpacking industry, before moving on to small-town American, where they spend time with the Amish community, cooking together and learning about their different way of life. Their first leg ends in St Louis, where they try 'concrete custard' - a treat so rich and thick it has to be served upside down(n)

programme.title: China: A New World Order
programme.desc: The concluding film explores the growing tension between China and the West over allegations that the Chinese state has engaged for years in large-scale industrial espionage and hacking to steal the West's business secrets and technological know-how. China denies any such wrongdoing - but growing anger in the US is fuelling the trade war with President Xi Jinping's superpower(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The five remaining contestants take part in a commercial design challenge to transform holiday lodges in the Lincolnshire woods. Split into a team of two and a team of three, the competitors embark on the biggest project of the series so far, designing a bedroom each, along with a shared kitchen-diner and an outdoor area. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan spends a week exploring Colombia with a local radio host and journalist, discovering how much of the country's reputation for violence is justified. He investigates a new trend for narcotourism, where visitors seek out places associated with drug lord Pablo Escobar, and finds out how Colombians feel about foreigners' fascination with their nation's most infamous criminal. He also visits a Jesus statue shop, learns to make coffee the traditional way, and discovers a game involving explosions(n)

programme.title: A Black and White Killing: The Case That Shook America
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mobeen Azhar investigates the murder of Larnell Bruce by a member of a white supremacist gang in Portland, and assesses the degree to which race played a role in the choice of victim. The investigation delves into the history of the seemingly liberal city, and uncovers a dark underbelly of racial tensions in the state of Oregon. The programme also examines the events of the trial, which were marked by clashes between anti-fascist groups and white supremacists on the streets. Featuring interviews with members of the militant groups and expert witnesses called on to testify(n)

programme.title: A Black and White Killing: The Case That Shook America
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mobeen Azhar seeks to understand the complications surrounding the lives and behaviour of both perpetrator Russell Courtier and victim Larnell Bruce, looking more closely into the nature and extent of European Kindred's influence on the wider community. He investigates the brainwashing effect that membership of Oregon's racist prison gangs can have on young men, before hearing the jury deliver its verdict on whether Courtier is a racist murderer(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Arit Anderson compete for the chance to bring a taste of the Mediterranean to Pat and Jeremy's boring square garden in Shoreham-by-Sea, West Sussex(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst's trip takes in the Shires, where Tim shares some interesting sandwich recipes and uncovers an old coffin, while Catherine finds some medals(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As Longmeadow radiates colour in the height of summer, Monty Don takes a range of cuttings to get plants for free and also shows how best to prune lavender. Carol Klein visits a garden in Devon to revel in its eryngiums, while the programme also meets a passionate gardener and his partner in Wiltshire who use their garden as inspiration for art(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain demonstrates time-saving recipes for an easy end to the day, including a zingy beetroot pasta, a salmon poke bowl packed with avocado, pickled red cabbage and nuts, and an indulgent chocolate mousse. On the west coast of Scotland, Nadiya joins the team at an independent salmon farm to see how they produce the nation's most popular fish(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Adam Fleming and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Families from Cowbridge, Glamorgan, and Hampshire sample a new way of life as they swap homes. After the trial, they reveal whether they enjoyed the experience and what they have learned(n)

programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank (1959)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning fact-based drama based on the famous journal of a German Jewish girl forced to go into hiding with her family from Nazis in an Amsterdam attic for two years. Starring Millie Perkins, Shelley Winters and Richard Beymer(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents previously unseen finds from the show's travels round the country, visiting Muncaster Castle, the Bowes Museum and Norwich Cathedral. Antiques experts include Kate Bateman, Paul Laidlaw, Jonathan Pratt, James Lewis and Thomas Plant, with items ranging from a pair of Lalique vases to a 9.5mm projector(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: The centre is plunged into chaos as the harshest winter storm in over 20 years hits. Battling through snow and ice the staff have to battle to help the wildlife affected by the cold snap. Documentary following life for staff and the animals they care for at the SSPCA's world-leading wildlife rescue centre(n)

programme.title: Golf: Solheim Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of day one of the women's team competition between Europe and USA, held this year at Gleneagles in Perthshire. The Americans had won the last two stagings of the tournament, but Europe had prevailed the last two times it had been held in Scotland, back in 1992 and 2000(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: An extended edition sees Monty Don add autumn perennials to his borders, show how to take root cuttings and reveal plans for next year when he plants bulbs for spring colour. On her Kent allotment, Frances Tophill catches up on some seasonal sowing and invites a special guest to help her with a small building project. Nick Bailey visits Cambridge University Botanic Garden, where he finds out how and why some plants produce their scent at the end of the day. The programme also returns to the wildlife garden of Kate Bradbury to find out about the lives of solitary bees(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: Bob is offered advice by his friend Paddy Chayefsky about his autobiographical film All That Jazz, and hears Gwen's account of their relationship with him and the making of Chicago. Years later Bob and Gwen reminisce in a bar and Bob tells her he has been offered to direct the revival of Sweet Charity and declined, but suggests that Gwen take the role of supervisor to recreate the original dance numbers. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star in the final episode of the biographical drama about the lives of choreographer-director Bob Fosse and celebrated Broadway dancer Gwen Verdon(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig delves into a bit of 'quintessential' QI, with panellists Alan Davies, Cariad Lloyd, Holly Walsh and Josh Widdicombe at the brunt of her quizzing. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Is Boris Johnson's Brexit strategy unravelling? The death of Robert Mugabe(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard and her panel of guests discuss how writers and artists are responding to anxiety in contemporary culture(n)

programme.title: A Late Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: The oldest member of a string quartet announces he is retiring due to ill health. His decision leads to hidden resentments among the others erupting, threatening to destroy years of friendship and artistry. Meanwhile, the teenage daughter of two of the musicians harbours secret desires for one of their colleagues. Drama, starring Christopher Walken, Philip Seymour Hoffman and Catherine Keener(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Great Irish Journeys with Martha Kearney
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues to trace the footsteps of 19th-century artist and geologist George Victor Du Noyer, who spent 35 years charting the landscape, people and buildings of Ireland. Martha travels north, taking in the extraordinary beauty of Dunluce Castle, and onwards to Mussenden Temple, pondering its unusual location. She also marvels at how Belfast was shaped by the Victorians, visits Kearney Point on the Ards Peninsula and journeys south to Dunmoe, which overlooks the site of the Battle of the Boyne(n)

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Blencathra
programme.desc: Award-winning film-maker Terry Abraham returns to the Lake District to showcase 'the people's mountain' - Blencathra, looking at the lives of local residents, schoolchildren and tourists. Featuring contributions from comedian Ed Byrne, broadcaster Stuart Maconie, mountaineer Alan Hinkes OBE and record-breaking fell runner Steve Birkinshaw(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez looks back at the discovery of three extraordinary pre-war treasures, and the explorer-archaeologists who came across them. This time, she examines the discovery of the 40,000-year-old artwork known as The Lion Man, found in a remote cave in Germany in 1939 by Nazi party member and archaeologist, Robert Wetzel(n)

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the history of the Norse warriors, delving into their customs and searching for the truth behind their fearsome reputation. He begins by telling the story of their origins, and investigates the world of their ancient ancestors. The programme charts Scandinavia's prehistoric landscape, from the Baltic Sea to Norway's Atlantic coast, and Neil examines archaeological evidence to create a portrait of the society from which the Vikings emerged(n)

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb explores the hidden dangers of properties built in the Tudor period by looking at the new style of multi-room homes that only the aristocracy had previously been able to afford, which were now being constructed by the emerging wealthy classes. Plus, the presenter looks at the exotic new goods brought in from the New World by merchants that could actually be deadly(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how British Rail transformed the network after the Second World War, changing from steam to diesel and electric engines, and creating modern stations such as London's Euston. Featuring archive British Transport Films footage. Narrated by Rebecca Front(n)

programme.title: Patrick Kielty's Mulholland Drive
programme.desc: Patrick Kielty's journey to find the truth about Belfast-born William Mulholland, who was responsible for building Los Angeles' water infrastructure and providing a water supply that allowed the city to grow into one of the largest in the world. Patrick's family all worked for the water company in Northern Ireland and upon discovering that the man who gave LA its water was from Belfast, decided to find out why he isn't globally recognised(n)

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Blencathra
programme.desc: Award-winning film-maker Terry Abraham returns to the Lake District to showcase 'the people's mountain' - Blencathra, looking at the lives of local residents, schoolchildren and tourists. Featuring contributions from comedian Ed Byrne, broadcaster Stuart Maconie, mountaineer Alan Hinkes OBE and record-breaking fell runner Steve Birkinshaw(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Professor Chris Lintott visits the UK Astronomy Technology Centre in Edinburgh, to see a camera designed to take pictures of exoplanets, and journeys through exoplanet discoveries(n)

programme.title: Maps: Power, Plunder and Possession
programme.desc: Professor Jerry Brotton examines the way maps have reflected contemporary politics and belief - and in some cases inspired them. He studies medieval religious cartography on maps showing pilgrims the routes to Jerusalem or heaven, Victorian illustrations of the world - with every nation awarded a score according to how 'civilised' they were deemed to be - and modern mapping of social problems including infant mortality and HIV(n)

programme.title: Lost Films of WW2
programme.desc: Part two of two. Footage shot by an officer on HMS Kent during the Battle for the Mediterranean, and the D-Day landings filmed by a naval lieutenant on his personal camera(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Pilot: The Extraordinary Story of Captain Winkle Brown
programme.desc: Former Royal Navy officer and test pilot Captain Eric 'Winkle' Brown has flown more types of aircraft than anyone in history and is regarded as one of the best pilots Britain has produced. In this programme, the 95-year-old chats to James Holland about his flying experiences and encounters with Nazis, as well as other adventures in the run-up to and during the Second World War, illustrated by archive and personal photographs(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund receives a visit from the bloodthirsty Bishop of Bath and Wells, who threatens to do him a mischief with a red-hot poker over the matter of an unpaid loan. As he attempts to raise the £1,000 he needs, he finds himself being constantly summoned to the court by the Queen. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson and Miranda Richardson(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Lord Melchett challenges Edmund to a drinking contest that he cannot get out of - but his puritanical uncle and aunt Lord and Lady Whiteadder are visiting on the same night, leaving him trying to be in two places at once. Comedy, guest starring Miriam Margolyes, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry(n)

programme.title: Blackadder

Unfortunately for them, the Queen decides to pay only one of the ransom demands. Comedy, guest starring Hugh Laurie, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry(n)

programme.title: Tom Waits: Tales from a Cracked Jukebox
programme.desc: Profile of the influential and inventive singer-songwriter, whose work encompasses a mixture of musical styles, from blues to Weimar cabaret. The programme incorporates rare archive recordings and interviews, and features contributions from Terry Gilliam, Ian Rankin and Ute Lemper(n)

programme.desc: At the Royal Albert Hall, Matthew Baynton, Nicholas Collon and the Aurora Orchestra present Berlioz's Symphonie Fantastique from memory in a specially devised production incorporating elements of theatre, spectacular lighting and choreography as well as the composer's own words about his music. Introduced by Tom Service and Jess Gillam(n)

programme.title: Showbands: How Ireland Learned to Party
programme.desc: The showband was a uniquely Irish phenomenon. It was a movement that saw thousands of young people travel up and down the country in the late '50s, '60s and early '70s to the 'Dancehalls of Romance' to be entertained by the Royal Showband, the Miami Showband and Big Tom and the Mainliners, and individuals including Dickie Rock, Joe Dolan and Brendan Bowyer. Ardal O'Hanlon looks back at the phenomenon, examining what triggered the infamous era, the people involved, and its eventual end in the 1980s(n)

programme.title: Totally British: 70s Rock 'n' Roll
programme.desc: Part one of two. A collection of archive performances by artists including David Bowie, Free, Status Quo, Mott the Hoople, the Faces, the Sensational Alex Harvey Band, Humble Pie, Man, Babe Ruth and Thin Lizzy from TV shows such as The Old Grey Whistle Test and Top of the Pops(n)

programme.desc: On a hot day, lots of people decide to visit a tropical lake and there's a traffic jam. It looks like Duggee and the Squirrels might be stuck for some time, but thankfully Duggee has his traffic badge and knows what to do(n)

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz needs to send two stranded aliens back to space(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Mittens befriends Cotton-tail, their playful antics lead to trouble. Now Peter must race to save his friend and sister from the clutches of Old Brown(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world(n)

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: As newborn baby sea turtles make their way to the ocean, the Octonauts must steer them clear a giant wave and hungry predators(n)

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: Pre-school live action series featuring a cast of youngsters pretending to be grown-ups in an imaginary town. Narrated by Eamonn Holmes(n)

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Robert brings four dusters to life to help dust and tidy around Justin's House. However, the mischievous Dusterlings soon go rogue, making a mess and tickling anyone that moves(n)

programme.title: Tee and Mo

programme.title: Yakka Dee!

programme.title: Alphablocks

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula paint a giant mural at Amma's nursery, but Bing's rainbow colours spoil Sula's tree design and she is upset that her hard work has been ruined(n)

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: Things get out of hand when Chickedy and Chick try to turn the pedals of a bike. The Twirlywoo Screen provides the Twirlywoos with a cycle of their own, but it proves difficult to control(n)

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin Fletcher goes on an outdoor adventure with one of his friends, building a den, meeting an owl, and singing around a campfire(n)

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po receive a call on the Tubby Phone. It's the Tiddlytubbies who ring to say hello, just before the Tubby Phone Dance(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with bath toys and enjoy songs and story time together(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day reveals the different ways in which animals sleep, revealing how many young apes must learn how to make a bed out of leaves. In Tibet, bar-headed geese make a soft fluffy nest out of feathers on the hard rock, while Meerkats spend the night in burrows underground(n)

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: The monsters make Blushberry Blobcakes with Mr Marrow. They gather magical Blushberries from the nature garden and each make a special wish as they stir the cake mixture(n)

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we join four-year-old Alexa, her little brother Archie, and Dad as they visit their friends for a weekend in the countryside. Alexa leads everyone on a walk to the forest and they all get stuck in the mud as they splosh through the fields. Later, Alexa and friends enjoy a sleep-over on a big bouncy blow-up bed! They finish their weekend together with a trip to the seaside. Alexa has fun collecting pebbles on a beach but will she be able to run away from the waves so her feet don't get soggy?(n)

programme.desc: Messy monster cannot find his caterpillar friends, and the garden bush where he left his friends is now covered with pretty butterflies(n)

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Rally driver Catie shows off three amazing off-road machines. She rides a quad bike, shows us an amazing space exploration vehicle and then drives a tank(n)

programme.desc: A big storm wrecks the rabbits' treehouse, and placing it back together leads to a big adventure(n)

programme.title: The Welsh Italians
programme.desc: Michela Chiappa concludes her documentary with a visit to the Italian town of Bardi, where her family and many other Welsh-Italians came from. The place is buzzing with Welsh people visiting for a week-long festival, culminating in a street party to celebrate the immigrants who return each year(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at the Ford factory in Dagenham, Essex(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: Driven: The Billy Monger Story
programme.desc: Documentary following 18-year-old Billy Monger as he attempts to get back into motor racing after being involved in a crash in April 2017 that resulted in the amputation of his legs. With the support of the racing community and his hero Lewis Hamilton, Billy will have to change the rules of motorsport and learn to drive again without legs, all while coming to terms with life as a person with a disability(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines how supermarkets are fighting to bring consumers their food on demand, and finds out how Ocado are using robots to revolutionise online delivery. Elsewhere in the programme, he also visits a state of the art lettuce farm in Norfolk which supplies 300 million lettuces a year, and Nikki Fox tests out the Co-op's new pay in the aisle technology as we move towards a checkout-free future(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers investigate the sale of knives to under-18s. They also catch up with a Lamborghini-driving fraudster who has so many aliases he loses track of who he is supposed to be(n)

programme.desc: An animal-lover falls down a sewer trying to rescue his dog and a 60-year-old woman trying to row the Atlantic finds herself in jeopardy when her boat fills with water(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Arit Anderson compete for the chance to bring a taste of the Mediterranean to Pat and Jeremy's boring square garden in Shoreham-by-Sea, West Sussex(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst's trip takes in the Shires, where Tim shares some interesting sandwich recipes and uncovers an old coffin, while Catherine finds some medals(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby is left distraught after Rainie reveals the truth about her night with Ian and decides to leave the Square. But worse is to come. Daniel tries to distract Jean ahead of her operation, Chantelle's nerves threaten her chances of winning the competition and Phil overhears Sonia speaking to Bianca about barely seeing her and sends them an anonymous gift(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox return with the consumer show. They investigate allegations about a safety device in millions of homes and rank energy companies on cost, complaints and customer service. Pixie Lott exposes how high street chains are failing on the basics of recycling, and in Rogue Traders, Matt confronts a gardening firm whose elaborate lies have left customers thousands of pounds out of pocket(n)

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: 'Odd One Out'
programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. In this personal documentary, the singer opens up about the impact cyberbullies had on her life and the effect on her mental health. Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second edition of the topical debate - originally billed as the first of a new series until another episode was sneaked in last week. As usual she will be inviting the studio audience - this time a venue in Norwich - to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: When Andrew Neil decided to step down from This Week back in July, the BBC decided that rather than continue with a new host, they would find a different show to take its place. And here it is - although fans of Radio 5 Live will already be familiar with Brexitcast, which has become hugely popular since its launch in 2017. This TV version sees the same team, Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason, engaging in light-hearted gossip about all the latest goings-on in the process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley meets the volunteer teams who carry out rescues in the caves and mines of the Peak District in Derbyshire. Rav Wilding has the latest appeals and CCTV footage(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, to see a commercial space, and in Kent meets a couple who are returning to property development after taking a few years out. Dion Dublin meets a husband and wife team at a split-level flat in London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Trading Standards Officers search the home of a loan shark and find a safe containing thousands of pounds. They also force a rogue builder to pay compensation to his elderly victims. Narrated by Harry Hepple(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles introduces more footage of near misses caught on CCTV, meeting the people who survived the serious accidents. This episode features a paraglider who crashed into a wall in the Peak District, a cyclist who smashed face-first onto the pavement witnessed by her son, and a cameraman who while filming a storm almost ended up a victim himself(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles starts this programme at the British Bird of Prey centre in the National Botanic Garden of Wales and asks the people of Carmarthenshire to help identify a mystery object. The red and blue teams then go shopping with experts Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt, hoping to find items of value to take to auction in Cardiff(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara tries to be the bigger person when she finds out about Daniel and Becky's plans, while Valerie helps her neighbour when his selfless act is tarnished by the ultimate betrayal(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson crosses the Solent to property shop on the Isle of Wight with a couple who have a £750,000 budget to find their dream island home with holiday-let potential. During his journey, the presenter also gets an intimate glimpse into 19th-century royal life when he visits Osbourne House, one of Queen Victoria's favourite homes(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold Hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In Pembrokeshire, Kate meets the last master thatcher based in Wales. She also helps with the harvesting of water reeds - an invasive, thick grass-like plant now used on 80 per cent of thatched roofs in the UK. In Pendine Sands, Kate joins a cross-country hunt adapted for modern times and struggles to stay on the horse as they chase hounds who have been specifically trained to only pick up on human scent. Finally, Kate and Teg visit an agricultural college that helps neuro-diverse students thrive through a deep connection with the land(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England goalkeeper David James, Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Elinor Barker, Ryder Cup golfer Andy Sullivan and rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is unsure how to deal with Bobby and things only get worse when Max tells him he has agreed to let his son stay with him. Chantelle makes it to the final two in the competition - only to face a big decision. After her operation, Jean is overjoyed when Kat gets Stacey on the phone. But then Daniel arrives with devastating news(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The battle for a semi-final place reaches its climax as the four celebrities are tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by last year's finalists Martin Bayfield and Spencer Matthews and champion John Partridge. Only the best two contestants can come one step closer to winning the title of Celebrity MasterChef 2019. For those who don't make the grade, the journey ends here(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Mike and Karen try to get their relationship back on track after Mike's crab stall misdemeanour, while he finally finds Bigsy sleeping rough at the castle after going Awol. Meanwhile after the police visit Marion about the break in next door, Karen heads back to the salon to discover that Geraldine is under suspicion over the mystery of what happened to the contents of Mrs Bookham's handbag. Comedy written by Benidorm's Derren Litten, starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen trawls through the archives to examine leisure activities, featuring clips of pram races, roller-discos and jiving undertakers, full of characters from Wales' TV past(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with Lucy Porter, Romesh Ranganathan and Judge Robert Rinder competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including lavish proposals, school reunions and New Year's Eve(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Steve McAndrew brings his dying father to London so he can spend his final days in a nearby hospice, but how much time he can spend with his parent is anyone's guess - the Ucos team is hard at work investigating the cold case murder of 55-year-old interpreter Agnes Bradley. A DNA sample provides a link to a rebellious teenager, and the team wonders if the killer could be related to him. But a whole new can of worms is opened when investigators discover the lad's mother was raped - could the same person have killed Agnes? Gerry and Steve hope that interviewing her friends from a local chess club may shed new light on the matter, but it's a possible professional issue that could hold the key to unlocking the mystery. Denis Lawson and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: Driven: The Billy Monger Story
programme.desc: Documentary following 18-year-old Billy Monger as he attempts to get back into motor racing after being involved in a crash in April 2017 that resulted in the amputation of his legs. With the support of the racing community and his hero Lewis Hamilton, Billy will have to change the rules of motorsport and learn to drive again without legs, all while coming to terms with life as a person with a disability(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines how supermarkets are fighting to bring consumers their food on demand, and finds out how Ocado are using robots to revolutionise online delivery. Elsewhere in the programme, he also visits a state of the art lettuce farm in Norfolk which supplies 300 million lettuces a year, and Nikki Fox tests out the Co-op's new pay in the aisle technology as we move towards a checkout-free future(n)

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news with Amy Irons(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley meets the Yorkshire and Humber Marine Unit in Whitby, and Rav Wilding presents appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a flat in Forest Hill, London, and two terraced houses in Leeds and Nantwich, Cheshire, before speaking to their new owners(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers investigate the sale of knives to under-18s. They also catch up with a Lamborghini-driving fraudster who has so many aliases he loses track of who he is supposed to be(n)

programme.desc: An animal-lover falls down a sewer trying to rescue his dog and a 60-year-old woman trying to row the Atlantic finds herself in jeopardy when her boat fills with water(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin visits Shropshire in search of a country home for a pair of free spirits who have a £300,000 budget with which to finally lay down some roots. Jonnie also gets in the saddle on a ride through the Shropshire hills with a bespoke bike builder(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Arit Anderson compete for the chance to bring a taste of the Mediterranean to Pat and Jeremy's boring square garden in Shoreham-by-Sea, West Sussex(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst's trip takes in the Shires, where Tim shares some interesting sandwich recipes and uncovers an old coffin, while Catherine finds some medals(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby is left distraught after Rainie reveals the truth about her night with Ian and decides to leave the Square. But worse is to come. Daniel tries to distract Jean ahead of her operation, Chantelle's nerves threaten her chances of winning the competition and Phil overhears Sonia speaking to Bianca about barely seeing her and sends them an anonymous gift(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox return with the consumer show. They investigate allegations about a safety device in millions of homes and rank energy companies on cost, complaints and customer service. Pixie Lott exposes how high street chains are failing on the basics of recycling, and in Rogue Traders, Matt confronts a gardening firm whose elaborate lies have left customers thousands of pounds out of pocket(n)

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: 'Odd One Out'
programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. In this personal documentary, the singer opens up about the impact cyberbullies had on her life and the effect on her mental health. Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second edition of the topical debate - originally billed as the first of a new series until another episode was sneaked in last week. As usual she will be inviting the studio audience - this time a venue in Norwich - to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: When Andrew Neil decided to step down from This Week back in July, the BBC decided that rather than continue with a new host, they would find a different show to take its place. And here it is - although fans of Radio 5 Live will already be familiar with Brexitcast, which has become hugely popular since its launch in 2017. This TV version sees the same team, Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason, engaging in light-hearted gossip about all the latest goings-on in the process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley meets the volunteer teams who carry out rescues in the caves and mines of the Peak District in Derbyshire. Rav Wilding has the latest appeals and CCTV footage(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, to see a commercial space, and in Kent meets a couple who are returning to property development after taking a few years out. Dion Dublin meets a husband and wife team at a split-level flat in London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Trading Standards Officers search the home of a loan shark and find a safe containing thousands of pounds. They also force a rogue builder to pay compensation to his elderly victims. Narrated by Harry Hepple(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles introduces more footage of near misses caught on CCTV, meeting the people who survived the serious accidents. This episode features a paraglider who crashed into a wall in the Peak District, a cyclist who smashed face-first onto the pavement witnessed by her son, and a cameraman who while filming a storm almost ended up a victim himself(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles starts this programme at the British Bird of Prey centre in the National Botanic Garden of Wales and asks the people of Carmarthenshire to help identify a mystery object. The red and blue teams then go shopping with experts Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt, hoping to find items of value to take to auction in Cardiff(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara tries to be the bigger person when she finds out about Daniel and Becky's plans, while Valerie helps her neighbour when his selfless act is tarnished by the ultimate betrayal(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson crosses the Solent to property shop on the Isle of Wight with a couple who have a £750,000 budget to find their dream island home with holiday-let potential. During his journey, the presenter also gets an intimate glimpse into 19th-century royal life when he visits Osbourne House, one of Queen Victoria's favourite homes(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to West Bromwich, where Pauline recently bought a house complete with her very first garden. However, she has no idea what to do with the plot, which is currently full of weeds and random bits of old broken junk(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold Hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are joined by Arit Anderson to create an all-year-round garden in Byfleet, Surrey, for stylish octogenarian Gay to enjoy the birds and remember her late husband. As ever, the two design teams go head to head, so it remains to be seen whther newcomer Arit's garden, which uses repeating circle motifs to create interest in an otherwise small and featureless space and incorporates a curved wooden seat surrounded by bright flowers, will triumph(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England goalkeeper David James, Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Elinor Barker, Ryder Cup golfer Andy Sullivan and rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is unsure how to deal with Bobby and things only get worse when Max tells him he has agreed to let his son stay with him. Chantelle makes it to the final two in the competition - only to face a big decision. After her operation, Jean is overjoyed when Kat gets Stacey on the phone. But then Daniel arrives with devastating news(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The battle for a semi-final place reaches its climax as the four celebrities are tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by last year's finalists Martin Bayfield and Spencer Matthews and champion John Partridge. Only the best two contestants can come one step closer to winning the title of Celebrity MasterChef 2019. For those who don't make the grade, the journey ends here(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Mike and Karen try to get their relationship back on track after Mike's crab stall misdemeanour, while he finally finds Bigsy sleeping rough at the castle after going Awol. Meanwhile after the police visit Marion about the break in next door, Karen heads back to the salon to discover that Geraldine is under suspicion over the mystery of what happened to the contents of Mrs Bookham's handbag. Comedy written by Benidorm's Derren Litten, starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with Lucy Porter, Romesh Ranganathan and Judge Robert Rinder competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including lavish proposals, school reunions and New Year's Eve(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Steve McAndrew brings his dying father to London so he can spend his final days in a nearby hospice, but how much time he can spend with his parent is anyone's guess - the Ucos team is hard at work investigating the cold case murder of 55-year-old interpreter Agnes Bradley. A DNA sample provides a link to a rebellious teenager, and the team wonders if the killer could be related to him. But a whole new can of worms is opened when investigators discover the lad's mother was raped - could the same person have killed Agnes? Gerry and Steve hope that interviewing her friends from a local chess club may shed new light on the matter, but it's a possible professional issue that could hold the key to unlocking the mystery. Denis Lawson and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Having made it through a very wet winter, Sweeny now focuses on new life on the croft as he heads into spring. It is one of his favourite times of the year, and it's all go as one of his sows goes into labour, but this is one that he has to keep a close eye on. He also deals with the challenges of lambing; a time of very little sleep and plenty of early starts. With over 50 already lambed he's half way there, but his early morning checks prove vital as he finds out when he needs to give help to one of his sheep which is struggling. This isn't the only sheep he has to help. Sweeny lives on one of the most rugged coastlines in Britain, and although it's beautiful it can be very dangerous for the animals as he knows all to well. He calls the local mountain rescue in to help one of his sheep distressed on the cliff edge(n)
